class.name: HealthComponent
class.brief_description: Component managing the lifespan, damage mitigation, and health state of an entity.
class.description: [HealthComponent] tracks the current and maximum health of an in-game entity. It can function completely standalone (e.g., for breaking props or barrels) using a default baseline value, or automatically synchronize with a neighboring [StatsComponent] to scale its parameters dynamically. The component intercepts incoming flat damage via [method take_damage], automatically processes agility-based evasion or endurance-based mitigation rules if stats are available, and guarantees that vital structural lifecycle events like [signal died] or [signal health_changed] are safely emitted without redundant replication.

method.name: get_health
method.description: Returns the current health points remaining.

method.name: get_max_health
method.description: Returns the evaluated maximum health upper bound.

method.name: is_alive
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the current health is strictly greater than zero; otherwise returns [code]false[/code].

method.name: set_health
method.description: Updates the current health points. Clamps the value between [code]0.0[/code] and [member max_health] automatically. If the value changes, it triggers [signal health_changed]. If the value drops to zero or below from a living state, it fires the [signal died] signal.

method.name: set_max_health
method.description: Sets the maximum health pool ceiling. Clamps the input to a minimum threshold of [code]0.1[/code] to safeguard against division by zero. Readjusts current health proportionally based on the previous maximum capacity percentage.

method.name: take_damage
method.description: Applies raw incoming damage to the component. If a [StatsComponent] is detected on the same parent node, it processes an RNG-based roll against evasion chance first. If evasion fails, it scales down the final damage according to the entity's mitigation modifiers before reducing the health pool. Returns [code]true[/code] if damage calculation processed successfully, or [code]false[/code] if the entity was already dead or the input damage was invalid.

members.member: The fundamental baseline health of the node before any attribute coefficients or external flat and percent bonuses are computed. (Note: Ensure this is bound via ClassDB to populate in the Godot Inspector).
The entity's ongoing current health value, securely bounded within the maximum structural limits.
The evaluated active maximum health capacity, representing the unified sum of baseline metrics and dynamically calculated status parameters.

signal.name: died
signal.description: Fired exactly once at the precise moment when [member current_health] hits zero or below, shifting the entity state from alive to dead.

signal.name: health_changed
signal.description: Emitted whenever the entity's [member current_health] points shift up or down. Passes the newly calculated value ([param next_health]), the value prior to the modification ([param prev_health]) and ([param max_health]). This data is highly efficient for updating User Interface elements (like health bars) or calculating exact damage/healing deltas to spawn dynamic effects such as floating combat text without invoking manual C++ getter lookups.
